project_type: Client-side billing app using Firebase Firestore via CDN ES modules, deployed to GitHub Pages
language: JavaScript (ES modules via CDN, no build step)
build_system: None - static files served directly. No npm/node build pipeline. Firebase SDK loaded from gstatic.com CDN.
build_command: N/A - static files, no build step
test_command: N/A - no tests exist. Validate by AST parsing (node --check) for syntax errors.
verification_instructions: 1. Run `node --check billingappfiles/script.js` - will fail because of CDN imports (ESM from URLs). Instead, use a basic JS parser to confirm no syntax errors. 2. Manually verify the logic by reading the code.
setup_instructions: No setup needed - this is a static client-side app. Files are in billingappfiles/
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode. No build step. No test framework. The script.js uses ES module imports from CDN URLs which won't work in Node.js directly. Validation must be AST-based or manual.
contribution_requirements: No CONTRIBUTING.md exists. Follow existing code style (verbose formatting, explicit variable names).
key_patterns:
- Firebase SDK v10.12.2 loaded via CDN ESM imports from gstatic.com
- All state is in module-level variables (incomingBillCache, daybookCache, liveDraftsCache)
- onSnapshot listeners use docChanges() for incremental cache updates
- renderIncomingBills() already has a visibility guard (skips DOM rebuild when receiver hidden)
- renderDaybook() has NO visibility guard
- debouncedSaveDraft uses 300ms setTimeout
- syncLiveDraft is called directly (no separate debounce timer) alongside saveDraft
- India timezone (Asia/Kolkata) used for date formatting
- The app uses serverTimestamp() for createdAt on bills and daybook entries
- Product catalog loaded once from pricelist-a9d70 project's catalog/current doc
directory_structure:
billingappfiles/ - main app directory
  script.js - 4479 lines, all app logic
  bill.html - HTML structure
  style.css - styles
  pricelistfiles/ - separate pricelist management app
  productList.json - local product data (possibly unused, catalog comes from Firestore)
